translated: And her words weren't wrong at all... though her actions might have been.
Not a single word she said was wrong. In the scenario we just watched,
she was pretty aggressive during the actual recording of I-Haza. You might think,

"Why would she say something like that on a TV show?" but in her own way, she understands that I-Haza has viewers who look forward to idols having those deep, pointed conversations, and she does it to liven up the show.
She's working with Marina-chan, the MC, and the members of Cheerful*Days on the panel to make it exciting, so you can really feel her level of commitment.
She's a true professional.
Seriously.
It does make you nervous, though.
I was really on edge too, wondering what was going to happen,
but she's not a bad person.
She's just very straightforward, serious, and strict with both herself and others.
That's what I really wanted to talk about.
I ended up rambling quite a bit.
But...
I really felt that way.
So...
It's like this.
When you're working hard, your perspective becomes subjective.
You can only see what's right in front of you. In times like that, it'd be great if you could fly a drone camera way up high and get a wider, bird's-eye view of things.
If you do that, then even in moments where you're wondering "Why?"...
If you can both understand that the other person had their own position, or their own feelings, or that things happened while you weren't looking, then I'm sure you can reach an understanding.
I think it would help with communication too, so it's a really great lesson for daily life.
Yeah.
That's...
what I think.
This story was another turning point where Shizuku
took a step forward. We really got to see Shizuku's purity, her strength,
and just how cool she can be.
Also...
The title is "Beyond the Untangled Threads," and it uses the word "threads."
